sources.claim: Luoyang is represented as a synthetic region fixture for pipeline validation.
sources.notes: This supports fixture structure, not a shipped population estimate.

sources.claim: The fixture population is deliberately non-authoritative and disputed.
sources.notes: Retained separately to exercise disputed-source handling.

sources.claim: The commandery register supplies the Later Han province, commandery/kingdom, and county-rank name hierarchy used as the gameplay baseline.
sources.notes: Treated as an early-140s baseline, not a perfectly synchronized 191 census; dependent-state sections are outside this package scope.
sources.citation: Fan Ye, Book of Later Han, Treatise on Commanderies and States 1-5

sources.claim: The audited user workbook supplies corrected Hanja, Korean readings, source-cell identity, and retained 군/국-county hierarchy rows.
sources.notes: Historical facts and corrected names only. CHGIS/TGAZ coordinates, identifiers, record text, and licensed bulk data are not included in shipped content.
sources.citation: Later Han Administrative Units — Source Audit, 2026-07-13

sources.claim: Unicode Unihan kMandarin readings provide independently generated research-facing English romanization bases.
sources.notes: Generated readings are gameplay labels and may require later human correction for historical or polyphonic place-name readings. Unicode-3.0 license.

sources.claim: The DILA Place Authority TEI supplies attributed coordinate candidates used to place Later Han administrative anchors.
sources.notes: CC BY-SA 3.0 attribution and derivation metadata are retained in the portable snapshot. Direct, disambiguated, and inferred placements are distinguished; the complete upstream TEI is not redistributed.
sources.citation: Dharma Drum Institute of Liberal Arts, Buddhist Studies Place Authority Database, dataset dated through 2026-07-01

sources.claim: The map projection, inferred-anchor offsets, terrain decoration, and presentation geometry are project-authored stylizations around attributed coordinate candidates.
sources.notes: These are not surveyed administrative boundaries. Inferred anchors are visibly separated in the research snapshot from DILA-derived coordinates and may be refined without changing route mechanics.
sources.citation: Three Kingdoms SP-03 stylized Later Han layout
